task_completion.visual_evidence: A presentation software (e.g., LibreOffice Impress) is open with the file 'product_showcase.odp' visible in the title bar.

task_completion.description: Navigate to the second slide of the presentation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Slide 2 is selected in the left-hand slide pane and displayed in the main workspace, showing three product images.

task_completion.description: Select the Watch, Camera, and Headphones images.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Selection handles (bounding boxes) are simultaneously visible around the three product images (labeled WATCH, CAMERA, and HEADPHONES).

task_completion.description: Align the three images to their vertical centers.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The three images are perfectly aligned along their horizontal midlines. The VLM should look for the use of the 'Center Vertically' alignment tool or visually verify that the vertical centers of all three images form a straight horizontal line.

task_completion.description: Distribute the three images evenly horizontally.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The horizontal empty space (gap) between the Watch and Camera is visually identical to the gap between the Camera and Headphones. The VLM should look for the use of the 'Distribute Horizontally' tool or visually verify the equal spacing.
The 'Save' button (floppy disk icon) is clicked, a save shortcut (Ctrl+S) is used, or the 'modified' indicator (usually an asterisk in the title bar) disappears.

integrity.description: The agent used the graphical user interface to perform the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen clicking through the presentation software's menus, toolbars, or using keyboard shortcuts within the GUI, rather than using terminal commands to modify the file.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual presentation software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The LibreOffice Impress (or equivalent) application is clearly visible, not a text editor or a script modifying the underlying XML of the .odp file.

integrity.description: The agent did not use a script to bypass manual GUI alignment.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows open showing Python scripts or command-line tools (like zip/unzip or sed) being executed to manipulate the .odp file directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target file is located at '/home/ga/Documents/Presentations/product_showcase.odp'. Slide 2 (index 1) contains three images with uppercase text labels 'WATCH', 'CAMERA', and 'HEADPHONES'. The alignment and distribution must be highly precise (system tolerance is 0.05 cm), meaning the images should look perfectly centered vertically and perfectly evenly spaced horizontally to the naked eye.
